member.name: M:Foundatio.Messaging.RabbitMQMessageBus.GetRetryCountFromHeader(RabbitMQ.Client.Events.BasicDeliverEventArgs)
member.summary: For quorum queues: x-delivery-count is set by broker (1 on first redelivery, absent on the first attempt) For classic queues: x-delivery-count is only present if we added it during previous requeue

member.name: M:Foundatio.Messaging.RabbitMQMessageBus.PublishImplAsync(System.String,System.Object,Foundatio.Messaging.MessageOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Publish the message
member.remarks: RabbitMQ has an upper limit of 2GB for messages.BasicPublish blocking AMQP operations. The rule of thumb is: avoid sharing channels across threads. Publishers in your application that publish from separate threads should use their own channels. The same is a good idea for consumers.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Messaging.RabbitMQMessageBus.CreateConnectionAsync
member.summary: Connect to a broker - RabbitMQ

member.name: M:Foundatio.Messaging.RabbitMQMessageBus.ParseServerVersion(System.Collections.Generic.IDictionary{System.String,System.Object})
member.summary: Parses the RabbitMQ broker version from AMQP server properties (the version field is UTF-8 bytes).

member.name: M:Foundatio.Messaging.RabbitMQMessageBus.CreateDelayedExchangeAsync(RabbitMQ.Client.IChannel)
member.summary: Attempts to create the delayed exchange. On RabbitMQ 4.3+ the probe is skipped because the rabbitmq_delayed_message_exchange plugin depends on Mnesia which was removed. When the server version could not be determined, the probe is still attempted so the plugin is used if available.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Messaging.RabbitMQMessageBus.CreateQueueAsync(RabbitMQ.Client.IChannel)
member.summary: The client sends a message to an exchange and attaches a routing key to it. The message is sent to all queues with the matching routing key. Each queue has a receiver attached which will process the message. We’ll initiate a dedicated message exchange and not use the default one. Note that a queue can be dedicated to one or more routing keys.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Messaging.RabbitMQMessageBus.ParseHostEndpoint(System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Parses a host string in format "hostname" or "hostname:port" into an AmqpTcpEndpoint.

member.name: P:Foundatio.Messaging.RabbitMQMessageBusOptions.ConnectionString
member.summary: The connection string. See https://www.rabbitmq.com/uri-spec.html for more information. Provides credentials and vhost. When Hosts is specified, the host in the connection string is ignored.

member.name: P:Foundatio.Messaging.RabbitMQMessageBusOptions.Hosts
member.summary: List of hosts for failover. When specified, the client will try each host in order until one succeeds. Format: "hostname" or "hostname:port" (default port is 5672, or 5671 for amqps). If not specified, the host from ConnectionString is used.

member.name: P:Foundatio.Messaging.RabbitMQMessageBusOptions.DefaultMessageTimeToLive
member.summary: The default message time to live. The value of the expiration field describes the TTL period in milliseconds.

member.name: P:Foundatio.Messaging.RabbitMQMessageBusOptions.Arguments
member.summary: Arguments passed to QueueDeclare. Some brokers use it to implement additional features like message TTL.

member.name: P:Foundatio.Messaging.RabbitMQMessageBusOptions.IsDurable
member.summary: Durable (will survive a broker restart)

member.name: P:Foundatio.Messaging.RabbitMQMessageBusOptions.IsSubscriptionQueueExclusive
member.summary: Whether the subscription queue is exclusive to this message bus instance.

member.name: P:Foundatio.Messaging.RabbitMQMessageBusOptions.SubscriptionQueueAutoDelete
member.summary: Whether the subscription queue should be automatically deleted.

member.name: P:Foundatio.Messaging.RabbitMQMessageBusOptions.SubscriptionQueueName
member.summary: The name of the subscription queue this message bus instance will listen on.

member.name: P:Foundatio.Messaging.RabbitMQMessageBusOptions.AcknowledgementStrategy
member.summary: How messages should be acknowledged.

member.name: P:Foundatio.Messaging.RabbitMQMessageBusOptions.PrefetchCount
member.summary: Consumer prefetch count. Limits the number of unacknowledged messages a consumer can have. Set to 0 (default) which will prefetch all available messages (unbounded). Recommended values: 5-50 for most scenarios, higher for fast processing, lower for slow/heavy processing or large message sizes.

member.name: P:Foundatio.Messaging.RabbitMQMessageBusOptions.PrefetchSize
member.summary: Consumer prefetch size in bytes. Limits the total size of unacknowledged messages a consumer can have. Set to 0 (default) for no size limit. This provides additional flow control beyond message count.

member.name: P:Foundatio.Messaging.RabbitMQMessageBusOptions.GlobalQos
member.summary: Whether QoS settings apply globally to the connection or just to the channel. When true, the QoS settings apply to all consumers on the connection. When false (default), the QoS settings apply only to consumers on this channel.

member.name: P:Foundatio.Messaging.RabbitMQMessageBusOptions.DeliveryLimit
member.summary:
Sets the maximum number of times a message can be delivered (retried) before it is discarded. If using classic queues, we will republish the message with an x-delivery-count header and acknowledge the previous message.
Setting this to -1 means there is no limit on the number of deliveries.

member.name: P:Foundatio.Messaging.RabbitMQMessageBusOptions.PublisherConfirmsEnabled
member.summary: When true, PublishAsync waits for broker confirmation before returning. This guarantees the message reached the broker but adds latency per publish. Performance impact varies by workload - use async/pipelining patterns for best results. Default: false (fire-and-forget publishing for backward compatibility). See: https://www.rabbitmq.com/docs/confirms#publisher-confirms

member.name: M:Foundatio.Messaging.RabbitMQMessageBusOptionsBuilder.Hosts(System.String[])
member.summary: Sets the list of hosts for failover support. Replaces the host from ConnectionString.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Messaging.RabbitMQMessageBusOptionsBuilder.Hosts(System.Collections.Generic.IList{System.String})
member.summary: Sets the list of hosts for failover support. Replaces the host from ConnectionString.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Messaging.RabbitMQMessageBusOptionsBuilder.PublisherConfirmsEnabled(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Enables publisher confirms, which guarantees the message reached the broker. When enabled, PublishAsync waits for broker confirmation before returning. See: https://www.rabbitmq.com/docs/confirms#publisher-confirms
member.param: Whether to enable publisher confirms. Default: true.
member.returns: The builder instance for method chaining.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Messaging.RabbitMQMessageBusOptionsBuilder.UseQuorumQueues
member.summary: Configures the message bus for RabbitMQ quorum queues using recommended settings. Disables auto-delete and exclusive mode for subscription queues, and sets quorum-specific arguments based on the current DeliveryLimit value. Preserves any existing arguments.
member.returns: The builder instance for method chaining.
